mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the prior three months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces the relative attractiveness of cash, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal broad-based consumer and residential investment weakness that compounds into a slower quarterly growth outlook, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. Neither series alone carries the same signal strength as their joint decline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM < 0 AND HOUST MoM < 0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes produce forward 60d returns not statistically different from single-series decline episodes, or Rank IC for the joint signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, feeding directly into near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO months do not show higher mean forward returns than negative INDPRO months (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with unemployment already near cyclical lows, decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals the exhaustion of labor market slack and an approaching turning point, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in slowing future earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates while UNRATE remains below 4%
failure_condition: During late-cycle low-unemployment periods, decelerating payroll growth is followed by positive 60d forward returns in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the negative directional claim.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while CPI inflation simultaneously accelerates, the implied real policy rate tightens faster than nominal rates alone suggest, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect requires both series moving adversely together, not either alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY change accelerating
failure_condition: Joint tightening-plus-inflation episodes show forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from baseline, or Rank IC for the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in housing starts (HOUST) above a threshold reflects strengthening consumer confidence and forward-looking construction demand, which is quickly reflected in equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST surge instances are followed by negative or flat forward 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases across a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, an upward revision or rebound in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms that the economy is regaining trend output, reducing recession risk premia embedded in equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified post-shock recovery windows, GDPC1 rebounds are not associated with significantly higher forward 60d returns compared to the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05).
